heading: Straddles
label: 1092 Straddles

(B) which read as follows: “The Secretary shall prescribe regulations which specify the proper methods for clearly identifying a straddle as an identified straddle (and the positions comprising such straddle), which specify the rules for the application of this section for a taxpayer which fails to properly identify the positions of an identified straddle, and which specify the ordering rules in cases where a taxpayer disposes of less than an entire position which is part of an identified straddle.”Pub. A, title I, § 101(e)98 Stat. 619July 18, 1984“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to positions established after , in taxable years ending after such date.December 31, 1983“(2).—Special rule for offsetting position stockIn the case of any stock of a corporation formed or availed of to take positions in personal property which offset positions taken by any shareholder, the amendments made by this section shall apply to positions established on or after , in taxable years ending on or after such date.May 23, 1983“(3)(c).—SubsectionThe amendment made by subsection (c) [amending this section] shall apply to positions established after , in taxable years ending after such date.June 30, 1984“(4)(d).—SubsectionThe amendment made by subsection (d) [amending this section] shall apply to positions established after the date of the enactment of this Act in taxable years ending after such date.”Amendment by applicable to positions established after , in taxable years ending after that date, except as otherwise provided, see section 102(f), (g) of , set out as a note under .section 102(e)(2) of Pub. L. 98–369Pub. L. 98–369section 1256 of this titleJuly 18, 1984, (c), , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title I, § 103(b)98 Stat. 628Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(b)6.—Requirement That Regulations Be Issued WithinMonths After the Date of EnactmentThe Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate shall prescribe initial regulations under section 1092(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (including regulations relating to mixed straddles) not later than the date 6 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [].July 18, 1984“(c).—Effective Date of Regulations With Respect to Mixed StraddlesThe regulations described in subsection (b) with respect to the application of section 1233 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to mixed straddles shall not apply to mixed straddles all of the positions of which were established before .”January 1, 1984, , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. Effective Date, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–34, title V, § 50895 Stat. 333Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Aug. 13, 1981Oct. 22, 1986“(a).—In GeneralExcept as otherwise provided in this section, the amendments made by this title [enacting this section and sections 1234A and 1256 of this title, amending sections 263, 341, 1212, 1221, 1231, 1232, 1233, 1236, and 6653 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as a note under ] shall apply to property acquired and positions established by the taxpayer after , in taxable years ending after such date.section 1256 of this titleJune 23, 1981“(b)Identification Requirements.—“(1)Under section 1236 of code.—The amendments made by section 506 [amending ] shall apply to property acquired by the taxpayer after the date of the enactment of this Act [] in taxable years ending after such date.section 1236 of this titleAug. 13, 1981“(2)(e)(2)Under section 1256(c) of code.—Section 1256(e)(2)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by this title) shall apply to property acquired and positions established by the taxpayer after , in taxable years ending after such date.December 31, 1981“(c)23, 1981.—Election With Respect to Property Held on JuneIf the taxpayer so elects (at such time and in such manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate shall prescribe) with respect to all regulated futures contracts or positions held by the taxpayer on , the amendments made by this title shall apply to all such contracts and positions, effective for periods after such date in taxable years ending after such date. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term ‘regulated futures contract’ has the meaning given to such term by section 1256(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, and the term ‘position’ has the meaning given to such term by section 1092(d)(2) of such Code.”June 23, 1981

Plan Amendments Not Required Until January 1, 1989For provisions directing that if any amendments made by subtitle A or subtitle C of title XI [§§ 1101–1147 and 1171–1177] or title XVIII [§§ 1800–1899A] of require an amendment to any plan, such plan amendment shall not be required to be made before the first plan year beginning on or after , see , as amended, set out as a note under .Pub. L. 99–514section 1140 of Pub. L. 99–514section 401 of this titleJan. 1, 1989

Treatment of Certain Losses on Straddles Entered Into Before Effective Date of Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981, , , as amended by , title XVIII, § 1808(d), , , 2817, provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title I, § 10898 Stat. 630Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(a).—General RuleFor purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], in the case of any disposition of 1 or more positions—“(1)which were entered into before 1982 and form part of a straddle, and“(2)to which the amendments made by title V of the Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981 [, see Effective Date note above] do not apply,Pub. L. 97–34any loss from such disposition shall be allowed for the taxable year of the disposition if such loss is incurred in a trade or business, or if such loss is incurred in a transaction entered into for profit though not connected with a trade or business.“(b).—Loss Incurred in a Trade or BusinessFor purposes of subsection (a), any loss incurred by a commodities dealer in the trading of commodities shall be treated as a loss incurred in a trade or business.“(c).—Net Loss AllowedIf any loss with respect to a position described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) is not allowable as a deduction (after applying subsections (a) and (b)), such loss shall be allowed in determining the gain or loss from dispositions of other positions in the straddle to the extent required to accurately reflect the taxpayer’s net gain or loss from all positions in such straddle.“(d).—Other RulesExcept as otherwise provided in subsections (a) and (c) and in sections 1233 and 1234 of such Code, the determination of whether there is recognized gain or loss with respect to a position, and the amount and timing of such gain or loss, and the treatment of such gain or loss as long-term or short-term shall be made without regard to whether such position constitutes part of a straddle.“(e).—StraddleFor purposes of this section, the term ‘straddle’ has the meaning given to such term by section 1092(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as in effect on the day after the date of the enactment of the Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981 [], and shall include a straddle all the positions of which are regulated futures contracts.Aug. 13, 1981“(f).—Commodities DealerFor purposes of this section, the term ‘commodities dealer’ means any taxpayer who—“(1)at any time before , was an individual described in section 1402(i)(2)(B) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by this subtitle), orJanuary 1, 1982“(2)was a member of the family (within the meaning of section 704(e)(3) [now 704(e)(2)] of such Code) of an individual described in paragraph (1) to the extent such member engaged in commodities trading through an organization the members of which consisted solely of—“(A)1 or more individuals described in paragraph (1), and“(B)1 or more members of the families (as so defined) of such individuals.“(g).—Regulated Futures ContractsFor purposes of this section, the term ‘regulated futures contracts’ has the meaning given to such term by section 1256(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as in effect before the date of enactment of this Act []).July 18, 1984“(h).—SyndicatesFor purposes of this section, any loss incurred by a person (other than a commodities dealer) with respect to an interest in a syndicate (within the meaning of section 1256(e)(3)(B) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954]) shall not be considered to be a loss incurred in a trade or business.”

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Aug. 13, 1981Oct. 22, 1986“(a).—In GeneralExcept as otherwise provided in this section, the amendments made by this title [enacting this section and sections 1234A and 1256 of this title, amending sections 263, 341, 1212, 1221, 1231, 1232, 1233, 1236, and 6653 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as a note under ] shall apply to property acquired and positions established by the taxpayer after , in taxable years ending after such date.section 1256 of this titleJune 23, 1981“(b)Identification Requirements.—“(1)Under section 1236 of code.—The amendments made by section 506 [amending ] shall apply to property acquired by the taxpayer after the date of the enactment of this Act [] in taxable years ending after such date.section 1236 of this titleAug. 13, 1981“(2)(e)(2)Under section 1256(c) of code.—Section 1256(e)(2)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by this title) shall apply to property acquired and positions established by the taxpayer after , in taxable years ending after such date.December 31, 1981“(c)23, 1981.—Election With Respect to Property Held on JuneIf the taxpayer so elects (at such time and in such manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate shall prescribe) with respect to all regulated futures contracts or positions held by the taxpayer on , the amendments made by this title shall apply to all such contracts and positions, effective for periods after such date in taxable years ending after such date. Treatment of Certain Losses on Straddles Entered Into Before Effective Date of Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981, , , as amended by , title XVIII, § 1808(d), , , 2817, provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title I, § 10898 Stat. 630Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(a).—General RuleFor purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], in the case of any disposition of 1 or more positions—“(1)which were entered into before 1982 and form part of a straddle, and“(2)to which the amendments made by title V of the Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981 [, see Effective Date note above] do not apply,Pub. L. 97–34any loss from such disposition shall be allowed for the taxable year of the disposition if such loss is incurred in a trade or business, or if such loss is incurred in a transaction entered into for profit though not connected with a trade or business.“(b).—Loss Incurred in a Trade or BusinessFor purposes of subsection (a), any loss incurred by a commodities dealer in the trading of commodities shall be treated as a loss incurred in a trade or business.“(c).—Net Loss AllowedIf any loss with respect to a position described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) is not allowable as a deduction (after applying subsections (a) and (b)), such loss shall be allowed in determining the gain or loss from dispositions of other positions in the straddle to the extent required to accurately reflect the taxpayer’s net gain or loss from all positions in such straddle.“(d).—Other RulesExcept as otherwise provided in subsections (a) and (c) and in sections 1233 and 1234 of such Code, the determination of whether there is recognized gain or loss with respect to a position, and the amount and timing of such gain or loss, and the treatment of such gain or loss as long-term or short-term shall be made without regard to whether such position constitutes part of a straddle.“(e).—StraddleFor purposes of this section, the term ‘straddle’ has the meaning given to such term by section 1092(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as in effect on the day after the date of the enactment of the Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981 [], and shall include a straddle all the positions of which are regulated futures contracts.Aug. 13, 1981“(f).—Commodities DealerFor purposes of this section, the term ‘commodities dealer’ means any taxpayer who—“(1)at any time before , was an individual described in section 1402(i)(2)(B) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by this subtitle), orJanuary 1, 1982“(2)was a member of the family (within the meaning of section 704(e)(3) [now 704(e)(2)] of such Code) of an individual described in paragraph (1) to the extent such member engaged in commodities trading through an organization the members of which consisted solely of—“(A)1 or more individuals described in paragraph (1), and“(B)1 or more members of the families (as so defined) of such individuals.“(g).—Regulated Futures ContractsFor purposes of this section, the term ‘regulated futures contracts’ has the meaning given to such term by section 1256(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as in effect before the date of enactment of this Act []).July 18, 1984“(h).—SyndicatesFor purposes of this section, any loss incurred by a person (other than a commodities dealer) with respect to an interest in a syndicate (within the meaning of section 1256(e)(3)(B) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954]) shall not be considered to be a loss incurred in a trade or business.”

children.heading: Recognition of loss in case of straddles, etc.
children.label: a Recognition of loss in case of straddles, etc.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any loss with respect to 1 or more positions shall be taken into account for any taxable year only to the extent that the amount of such loss exceeds the unrecognized gain (if any) with respect to 1 or more positions which were offsetting positions with respect to 1 or more positions from which the loss arose.

children.heading: Carryover of loss
children.label: B Carryover of loss
children.text: Any loss which may not be taken into account under subparagraph (A) for any taxable year shall, subject to the limitations under subparagraph (A), be treated as sustained in the succeeding taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: paragraph (1) shall not apply with respect to positions comprising the identified straddle,

children.label: ii
children.text: if there is any loss with respect to any position of the identified straddle, the basis of each of the offsetting positions in the identified straddle shall be increased by an amount which bears the same ratio to the loss as the unrecognized gain with respect to such offsetting position bears to the aggregate unrecognized gain with respect to all such offsetting positions,

children.label: I
children.text: is reasonable, consistent with the purposes of this paragraph, and consistently applied by the taxpayer, and

children.label: II
children.text: results in an aggregate increase in the basis of such offsetting positions which is equal to the loss described in clause (ii), and

children.label: iv
children.text: any loss described in clause (ii) shall not otherwise be taken into account for purposes of this title.

children.label: I
children.text: the close of the day on which the straddle is acquired, or

children.label: II
children.text: such time as the Secretary may prescribe by regulations.

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent provided by regulations, the value of each position of which (in the hands of the taxpayer immediately before the creation of the straddle) is not less than the basis of such position in the hands of the taxpayer at the time the straddle is created, and

children.label: iii
children.text: which is not part of a larger straddle.

children.heading: Application to liabilities and obligations
children.label: C Application to liabilities and obligations
children.text: Except as otherwise provided by the Secretary, rules similar to the rules of clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (A) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph with respect to any position which is, or has been, a liability or obligation.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: D Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations or other guidance as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this paragraph. Such regulations or other guidance may specify the proper methods for clearly identifying a straddle as an identified straddle (and for identifying the positions comprising such straddle), the rules for the application of this section to a taxpayer which fails to comply with those identification requirements, the rules for the application of this section to a position which is or has been a liability or obligation, methods of loss allocation which satisfy the requirements of subparagraph (A)(iii), and the ordering rules in cases where a taxpayer disposes (or otherwise ceases to be the holder) of any part of any position which is part of an identified straddle.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of any position held by the taxpayer as of the close of the taxable year, the amount of gain which would be taken into account with respect to such position if such position were sold on the last business day of such taxable year at its fair market value, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of any position with respect to which, as of the close of the taxable year, gain has been realized but not recognized, the amount of gain so realized.

children.heading: Special rule for identified straddles
children.label: B Special rule for identified straddles
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2)(A)(ii), the unrecognized gain with respect to any offsetting position shall be the excess of the fair market value of the position at the time of the determination over the fair market value of the position at the time the taxpayer identified the position as a position in an identified straddle.

children.label: I
children.text: each position (whether or not part of a straddle) with respect to which, as of the close of the taxable year, there is unrecognized gain, and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of such unrecognized gain.

children.heading: Reports not required in certain cases
children.label: ii Reports not required in certain cases
I
children.text: to any position which is part of an identified straddle,

children.label: II
children.text: to any position which, with respect to the taxpayer, is property described in paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1221(a) or to any position which is part of a hedging transaction (as defined in section 1256(e)), or

children.label: III
children.text: with respect to any taxable year if no loss on a position (including a regulated futures contract) has been sustained during such taxable year or if the only loss sustained on such position is a loss described in subclause (II).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations with respect to gain or loss on positions which are a part of a straddle as may be appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section and section 263(g). To the extent consistent with such purposes, such regulations shall include rules applying the principles of subsections (a) and (d) of section 1091 and of subsections (b) and (d) of section 1233.

children.heading: Elective provisions in lieu of section 1233(d) principles
children.label: A Elective provisions in lieu of section 1233(d) principles
I
children.text: by straddle-by-straddle identification, or

children.label: II
children.text: by the establishment (with respect to any class of activities) of a mixed straddle account for which gains and losses would be recognized (and offset) on a periodic basis,

children.label: ii
children.text: such offsetting will occur before the application of section 1256, and section 1256(a)(3) will only apply to net gain or net loss attributable to section 1256 contracts, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the principles of section 1233(d) shall not apply with respect to any straddle identified under clause (i)(I) or part of an account established under clause (i)(II).

children.heading: Limitation on net gain or net loss from mixed straddle account
children.label: B Limitation on net gain or net loss from mixed straddle account

children.heading: Not more than 50 percent of net gain may be treated as long-term capital gain
children.label: i Not more than 50 percent of net gain may be treated as long-term capital gain
children.text: In no event shall more than 50 percent of the net gain from such account for any taxable year be treated as long-term capital gain.

children.heading: Not more than 40 percent of net loss may be treated as short-term capital loss
children.label: ii Not more than 40 percent of net loss may be treated as short-term capital loss
children.text: In no event shall more than 40 percent of the net loss from such account for any taxable year be treated as short-term capital loss.

children.heading: Authority to treat certain positions as mixed straddles
children.label: C Authority to treat certain positions as mixed straddles
children.text: The regulations prescribed under paragraph (1) may treat as a mixed straddle positions not described in section 1256(d)(4).

children.heading: Timing and character authority
children.label: D Timing and character authority
children.text: The regulations prescribed under paragraph (1) shall include regulations relating to the timing and character of gains and losses in case of straddles where at least 1 position is ordinary and at least 1 position is capital.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “straddle” means offsetting positions with respect to personal property.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A taxpayer holds offsetting positions with respect to personal property if there is a substantial diminution of the taxpayer’s risk of loss from holding any position with respect to personal property by reason of his holding 1 or more other positions with respect to personal property (whether or not of the same kind).

children.heading: Special rule for identified straddles
children.label: B Special rule for identified straddles
children.text: In the case of any position which is not part of an identified straddle (within the meaning of subsection (a)(2)(B)), such position shall not be treated as offsetting with respect to any position which is part of an identified straddle.

children.label: i
children.text: the positions are in the same personal property (whether established in such property or a contract for such property),

children.label: ii
children.text: the positions are in the same personal property, even though such property may be in a substantially altered form,

children.label: iii
children.text: the positions are in debt instruments of a similar maturity or other debt instruments described in regulations prescribed by the Secretary,

children.label: iv
children.text: the positions are sold or marketed as offsetting positions (whether or not such positions are called a straddle, spread, butterfly, or any similar name),

children.label: v
children.text: the aggregate margin requirement for such positions is lower than the sum of the margin requirements for each such position (if held separately), or

children.label: vi
children.text: there are such other factors (or satisfaction of subjective or objective tests) as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe as indicating that such positions are offsetting.

children.heading: Presumption may be rebutted
children.label: B Presumption may be rebutted
children.text: Any presumption established pursuant to subparagraph (A) may be rebutted.

children.heading: Exception for certain straddles consisting of qualified covered call options and the optioned stock
children.label: 4 Exception for certain straddles consisting of qualified covered call options and the optioned stock
i
children.text: all the offsetting positions making up any straddle consist of 1 or more qualified covered call options and the stock to be purchased from the taxpayer under such options, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such straddle is not part of a larger straddle,

children.heading: Qualified covered call option defined
children.label: B Qualified covered call option defined
i
children.text: such option is traded on a national securities exchange which is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission or other market which the Secretary determines has rules adequate to carry out the purposes of this paragraph,

children.label: ii
children.text: such option is granted more than 30 days before the day on which the option expires,

children.label: iii
children.text: such option is not a deep-in-the-money option,

children.label: iv
children.text: such option is not granted by an options dealer (within the meaning of section 1256(g)(8)) in connection with his activity of dealing in options, and

children.label: v
children.text: gain or loss with respect to such option is not ordinary income or loss.

children.heading: Deep-in-the-money option
children.label: C Deep-in-the-money option
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), the term “deep-in-the-money option” means an option having a strike price lower than the lowest qualified bench mark.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subparagraph, for purposes of subparagraph (C), the term “lowest qualified bench mark” means the highest available strike price which is less than the applicable stock price.

children.heading: Special rule where option is for period more than 90 days and strike price exceeds $50
children.label: ii Special rule where option is for period more than 90 days and strike price exceeds $50
I
children.text: which is granted more than 90 days before the date on which such option expires, and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to which the strike price is more than $50,

children.heading: 85 percent rule where applicable stock price $25 or less
children.label: iii 85 percent rule where applicable stock price $25 or less
I
children.text: the applicable stock price is $25 or less, and

children.label: II
children.text: but for this clause, the lowest qualified bench mark would be less than 85 percent of the applicable stock price,

children.heading: Limitation where applicable stock price $150 or less
children.label: iv Limitation where applicable stock price $150 or less
I
children.text: the applicable stock price is $150 or less, and

children.label: II
children.text: but for this clause, the lowest qualified bench mark would be less than the applicable stock price reduced by $10,

children.label: i
children.text: the qualified covered call options referred to in such subparagraph are closed or the stock is disposed of at a loss during any taxable year,

children.label: ii
children.text: gain on disposition of the stock to be purchased from the taxpayer under such options or gains on such options are includible in gross income for a later taxable year, and

children.label: iii
children.text: such stock or option was not held by the taxpayer for 30 days or more after the closing of such options or the disposition of such stock.

children.heading: Strike price
children.label: F Strike price
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “strike price” means the price at which the option is exercisable.

children.label: i
children.text: the closing price of such stock on the most recent day on which such stock was traded before the date on which such option was granted, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the opening price of such stock on the day on which such option was granted, but only if such price is greater than 110 percent of the price determined under clause (i).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: H Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this paragraph. Such regulations may include modifications to the provisions of this paragraph which are appropriate to take account of changes in the practices of option exchanges or to prevent the use of options for tax avoidance purposes.

children.heading: Personal property
children.label: 1 Personal property
children.text: The term “personal property” means any personal property of a type which is actively traded.

children.heading: Position
children.label: 2 Position
children.text: The term “position” means an interest (including a futures or forward contract or option) in personal property.

children.label: i
children.text: such stock is of a type which is actively traded and at least 1 of the positions offsetting such stock is a position with respect to such stock or substantially similar or related property, or

children.label: ii
children.text: such stock is of a corporation formed or availed of to take positions in personal property which offset positions taken by any shareholder.

children.heading: Rule for application
children.label: B Rule for application
children.text: For purposes of determining whether subsection (e) applies to any transaction with respect to stock described in subparagraph (A)(ii), all includible corporations of an affiliated group (within the meaning of section 1504(a)) shall be treated as 1 taxpayer.

children.heading: Positions held by related persons, etc.
children.label: 4 Positions held by related persons, etc.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In determining whether 2 or more positions are offsetting, the taxpayer shall be treated as holding any position held by a related person.

children.label: i
children.text: is the spouse of the taxpayer, or

children.label: ii
children.text: files a consolidated return (within the meaning of section 1501) with the taxpayer for any taxable year which includes a portion of such period.

children.heading: Certain flowthrough entities
children.label: C Certain flowthrough entities
children.text: If part or all of the gain or loss with respect to a position held by a partnership, trust, or other entity would properly be taken into account for purposes of this chapter by a taxpayer, then, except to the extent otherwise provided in regulations, such position shall be treated as held by the taxpayer.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: In the case of a straddle at least 1 (but not all) of the positions of which are section 1256 contracts, the provisions of this section shall apply to any section 1256 contract and any other position making up such straddle.

children.heading: Special rule for identified straddles
children.label: B Special rule for identified straddles
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(2) (relating to identified straddles), subparagraph (A) and section 1256(a)(4) shall not apply to a straddle all of the offsetting positions of which consist of section 1256 contracts.

children.heading: Section 1256 contract
children.label: 6 Section 1256 contract
children.text: The term “section 1256 contract” has the meaning given such term by section 1256(b).

children.heading: Position to include interest in certain debt
children.label: A Position to include interest in certain debt
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2), an obligor’s interest in a nonfunctional currency denominated debt obligation is treated as a position in the nonfunctional currency.

children.heading: Actively traded requirement
children.label: B Actively traded requirement
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), foreign currency for which there is an active interbank market is presumed to be actively traded.

children.heading: Special rules for physically settled positions
children.label: 8 Special rules for physically settled positions
A
children.text: terminated the position for its fair market value immediately before the settlement, and

children.label: B
children.text: sold the property so delivered by the taxpayer at its fair market value.

children.heading: Exception for hedging transactions
children.label: e Exception for hedging transactions
children.text: This section shall not apply in the case of any hedging transaction (as defined in section 1256(e)).

children.heading: Treatment of gain or loss and suspension of holding period where taxpayer grantor of qualified covered call option
children.label: f Treatment of gain or loss and suspension of holding period where taxpayer grantor of qualified covered call option

children.heading: Treatment of loss
children.label: 1 Treatment of loss
children.text: Any loss with respect to such option shall be treated as long-term capital loss if, at the time such loss is realized, gain on the sale or exchange of such stock would be treated as long-term capital gain.

children.heading: Suspension of holding period
children.label: 2 Suspension of holding period
children.text: The holding period of such stock shall not include any period during which the taxpayer is the grantor of such option.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: g Cross reference
children.text: For provision requiring capitalization of certain interest and carrying charges where there is a straddle, see section 263(g).
